name: OpenWeatherMap API
what_it_does: Comprehensive weather data API providing current conditions, forecasts, historical data, and weather alerts for any location worldwide via REST endpoints.
use_cases: Getting current weather and forecasts for agent decision-making
Historical weather data analysis and climate research
Displaying weather widgets in apps and dashboards
not_for: Real-time lightning/radar data at high resolution
Aviation-grade weather (use specialized aviation weather APIs)
best_when: You need reliable, globally comprehensive weather data with a generous free tier and simple API integration.
avoid_when: You need very high accuracy hyperlocal forecasts or aviation/maritime precision weather data.

auth.notes: Single API key passed as appid query parameter. Simple key-based access with no scopes.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 1000 calls/day, current weather and 5-day forecast
pricing.notes: Generous free tier covers most small to medium use cases. One Call API 3.0 now requires credit card but has a free 1000 calls/day.

performance.rate_limits: 60 calls/minute on free; higher on paid plans

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors include cod (status code) and message fields. Some error messages are generic.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read-only API - all calls are naturally idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: API key activation takes up to 2 hours after creation - new keys fail with 401 initially
One Call API 3.0 (newer unified API) now requires credit card registration despite free tier
Free tier has both daily and per-minute rate limits - minute limit is the common gotcha
Forecast data uses Unix timestamps - agents must handle timezone conversion carefully
Different API versions (2.5 vs 3.0) have different endpoint structures and response formats
